title: NCAA eases first-time betting penalties while reinforcing competition integrity safeguards
excerpt: NCAA Division III council reduced first-time betting penalties while reaffirming integrity amid recent gambling manipulation investigations.
content_html:
The NCAA Division III Management Council has approved new sports-betting reinstatement guidelines that reduce penalties in some cases while keeping a firm focus on protecting competition. The change takes effect immediately and applies only to first-time violations that do not involve concerns about the integrity of games or attempts to influence outcomes.

Under the revised policy, eligible student-athletes can receive reduced penalties if they complete an appropriate clinical evaluation or support program. Council members said the update is designed to encourage athletes to seek help when gambling becomes a problem without weakening the NCAA’s long-standing commitment to fair competition.

NCAA responds to changing sports betting challenges 

The decision comes as college sports continue to face growing scrutiny over gambling-related risks. Earlier this year, federal prosecutors unveiled an alleged international point-shaving scheme involving NCAA Division I men’s basketball and professional games in China. Investigators allege the operation relied on bribery, wire fraud and manipulated performances to exploit point-spread betting, highlighting how difficult those markets can be to monitor because of their high betting volume and unpredictable results.

Integrity experts have said suspicious activity is usually identified through unusual betting patterns rather than the size of individual wagers, while cross-border betting remains especially challenging because overseas operators often do not share data with U.S. monitoring systems. The case has also renewed calls for stronger athlete education and greater international cooperation to detect manipulation before it spreads across competitions.

The NCAA has also recently reaffirmed its ban on sports wagering by student-athletes and athletics staff. A proposal that briefly would have allowed betting on professional sports was rescinded after Division I member schools voted to restore the previous rule, leaving the prohibition in place across all three NCAA divisions.

Enforcement efforts have continued as well. In separate cases announced previously, six former men’s basketball student-athletes from New Orleans, Mississippi Valley and Arizona State were ruled permanently ineligible after investigations found betting-related game manipulation or the sharing of inside information with known bettors.

Alongside the reinstatement changes, the Division III council approved sponsorship of legislation to establish a National Collegiate women’s flag football championship, with a potential inaugural event planned for spring 2028 if all NCAA divisions adopt the proposal. Members also supported legislation allowing prospective student-athletes to sign limited agreements with agents before enrolling to help identify future playing opportunities.

The council also approved Division III budgets for the 2027 and 2028 fiscal years and the division’s 2026-27 strategic plan, both of which now head to the Presidents Council for final review in August. Leadership elections, strategic recommendations and continued exploration of an Athletic Trainer Connect program were also approved during the meeting.

Featured image: NCAA

sentences.en: Leadership elections, strategic recommendations and continued exploration of an Athletic Trainer Connect program were also approved during the meeting.
sentences.zh: 会议期间还批准了领导层选举、战略建议和对运动教练连接计划的继续探索。

sentences.en: Featured image: NCAA
sentences.zh: 特色图片：NCAA

content_html_zh:
NCAA 第三级管理委员会批准了新的体育博彩恢复指南，该指南减少了某些情况下的处罚，同时坚定地致力于保护竞争。 该更改立即生效，仅适用于不涉及对游戏完整性的担忧或试图影响结果的首次违规行为。

根据修订后的政策，符合条件的学生运动员如果完成适当的临床评估或支持计划，可以获得减轻的处罚。 委员会成员表示，此次更新旨在鼓励运动员在赌博成为问题时寻求帮助，同时又不会削弱 NCAA 对公平竞争的长期承诺。

NCAA 应对不断变化的体育博彩挑战

这一决定是在大学体育运动继续面临与赌博相关风险日益严格的审查之际做出的。 今年早些时候，联邦检察官公布了一项涉嫌国际削分计划，涉及 NCAA 第一级男子篮球和在中国举行的职业比赛。 调查人员指控，该活动依靠贿赂、电汇欺诈和操纵表现来利用点差投注，这突显出这些市场由于投注量高且结果不可预测而难以监控。

诚信专家表示，可疑活动通常是通过不寻常的投注模式而不是个人投注的规模来识别的，而跨境投注仍然特别具有挑战性，因为海外运营商通常不与美国监控系统共享数据。 此案还再次呼吁加强运动员教育和加强国际合作，以便在操纵行为在比赛中蔓延之前发现这一现象。

NCAA 最近还重申禁止学生运动员和体育工作人员进行体育博彩。 在一级联盟成员学校投票恢复之前的规则后，一项曾短暂允许职业体育博彩的提案被撤销，这项禁令在 NCAA 的所有三个联盟中仍然有效。

执法工作也在继续进行。 在此前宣布的其他案件中，来自新奥尔良、密西西比河谷和亚利桑那州立大学的六名前男子篮球学生运动员在调查发现与投注相关的比赛操纵或与已知投注者分享内幕信息后被裁定永久失去参赛资格。

除了恢复调整之外，第三级理事会还批准了立法赞助建立全国大学女子腰旗橄榄球锦标赛，如果所有 NCAA 分区都采纳该提案，则计划于 2028 年春季举行首届赛事。 成员们还支持立法，允许未来的学生运动员在报名之前与经纪人签署有限的协议，以帮助确定未来的比赛机会。

该委员会还批准了第三部门 2027 和 2028 财年的预算以及该部门的 2026-27 战略计划，这两项计划现已提交总统委员会于 8 月进行最终审查。 会议期间还批准了领导层选举、战略建议和对运动教练连接计划的继续探索。

特色图片：NCAA
